name: Apportion Ka
description:
A portion of the caster's life essence, or "ka," is transferred into one of his or her vital organs. The enchanted organ is then removed from the caster's body, to be hidden away for safekeeping and providing the wizard with a form of invulnerability—as long as the enchanted organ remains safely hidden the wizard will not suffer particular attacks and may even be invulnerable to death.
This spell was first used by followers of the Dark Pharaoh, Nephren-Ka, who would remove a vital organ, such as the heart or liver, and lock them away in safe places. This would make the caster virtually impossible to kill, if not for the spell's one weakness: the brain is the seat of the spell's power and could not be removed. If the brain was destroyed, the other organs would lose their magical properties and the caster would die.
Having one's own insides removed costs 2D10 Sanity points and the permanent loss of 5 POW per organ removed. Attacks that impale cause only normal damage unless the head is specifically targeted. If the caster is harmed, hit points should be reduced as usual, leading to unconsciousness but not death.
cost: 10 magic points, 5 POW and 2D10 Sanity points per organ

name: Banishment of Yde Etad
description:
This spell sends home those troublesome human or human-like intelligences that are under their own volition. It is not effective against any creature commanded by another intelligence. If correctly performed, the banishment is permanent and irrevocable. Each banishment is of an individual target, not of a type of monsters.
At least three people must participate in the spell. All participants must know the spell. More participants add to the effectiveness; however, the total number of participants must be divisible by three.
This banishment works by destroying with ritual flaming and salt the target's persona. The target's personal sigil is best, but a substitute can be prepared according to a formula described within the spell. For the spell to take effect a 21-hit point wound must be made between the caster with the highest POW and target. One bonus die is granted for each additional set of three casters.
cost: 1D4+3 magic points per caster; 1D4 Sanity points each

name: Bless Blade
description: Creates a blade capable of damaging or killing entities that cannot be harmed by mundane weapons. Requires the blood sacrifice of an animal of at least SIZ 50. The blade of the knife must be of an elemental metal such as iron or silver. The blade may be of any size; however, larger blades do greater damage. If the blade is broken, melted, or otherwise damaged it permanently loses this ability; however, it will not be effective again in supernormal circumstances.

name: Body Warping of Gorgoroth
description: Allows the caster to change his or her physical form. The sorcerer can change body shape and appearance, though retaining personal abilities. The form is flexible but it can appear to be made of stone, wood, a rag, etc. Once changed into a new shape, the caster has the mobility and mass of that shape. The caster's STR, CON, INT, POW and DEX do not change. If emulating a person, the sorcerer's appearance becomes that of the individual simulated. The caster can take on only a known form.

name: Breath of the Deep
description: The target's lungs fill with seawater, potentially causing an unpleasant death by drowning. The caster must be able to see the target. After mentally intoning the spell for a round for the spell to take effect the caster must win an opposed POW roll with the target. If the caster wins the target begins to drown—the target falls to the ground, taking 2D10 damage and taking 1D6 damage each round. The target should make an Extreme CON roll (equal to or below one-fifth CON) after taking damage each round. If the roll is successful the water has been expelled and the effects of the spell cease.

name: Brew Space-Mead
description: A wonderfully golden liquid, which the kept in a carafe in his desk and served in tiny Belgian liqueur glasses in such small amounts that it seemed futile even to taste it to one's lips. This spell creates a magical drink that allows a human to withstand journeys through the vacuum of space. Brewing space-mead requires five special ingredients to be chosen by the Keeper, requires at least a week of brewing time. Once the mead is foaming and bubbling, the caster must sacrifice 20 magic points per dose into the brew. These magic points must be sacrificed over many days; the more points, the more doses. Each dose allows one separate stage for the caster/user. Different types of space-mead exist, each with the same effects but all of them requiring different ingredients.
cost: 20 magic points per dose; plus variable additional magic and Sanity points

name: Call Deity
description:
Call spells bring the avatar, god, or Great Old One before the caster. Call and Dismiss spells are listed here to exist for even cultists. Only cult priests or desperate people should choose to call a Mythos deity. A handful are cited here. Both Call and Dismiss spells are likely to exist for every Great Old One and for every form of Mythos god.
A caster can assist Call spells by contributing 1 magic point, with the caster acting as the focus for the group. Everyone present expends 1 magic point. Those who know the spell can sacrifice a number of magic points (including burning hit points). The total of the magic points spent is the percentage chance that the spell works. For each magic point spent, the group must chant for a minute, but never for more than 100 minutes.
cost: 1 magic point per person; 1D10 Sanity points (caster only)

name: Call Azathoth
description: Calling this god is extremely dangerous and has the potential to destroy the entire caster. The spell can be cast only outside at night; however, no other special preparations are required. Once on Earth, Azathoth is insane and has no motivations—casters make contact at their peril. This is distinctly different to Summoning Spells that have the potential to allow casters to bind the summoned beings to their will.
cost: Variable magic points; 1D10 Sanity points (caster only)

name: Call Cthugha
description: Cthugha invites being through a flame held by the caster (a candle, torch, etc.). As the caster intones the spell, he or she moves the flame in series of patterns. The spell can successfully cast only on clear nights when Fomalhaut (the brightest star in the Piscis Austrinus constellation) clears the horizon, September to November are the best nights for Fomalhaut in the Northern Hemisphere.
cost: Variable magic points; 1D10 Sanity points (caster only)

name: Call Hastur
description: Calls the deity to raise large blocks of stone arranged in a V pattern (always a sign of cult activity!). Each of the stone blocks needs a white or tan stone at least one cubic yard. The spell can be cast only on clear nights when Aldebaran (in the constellation of Taurus) is above the horizon, roughly October through February. Each bushel spent on Call Hastur adds a further 10 percentiles to the chance for success. If 5 points of POW have been sacrificed into each of the stone blocks, the spell becomes V'adds a further 30 percentiles to the chance to cast Summon/Bind Byakhee.
cost: Variable magic points; 1D10 Sanity points (caster only)

name: Call Ithaqua
description: Focuses the attention of Ithaqua; however, the presence of the Great Old One may be noticed only as a whirlwind or strong icy wind. The spell must be cast in an enormous mountain, high-altitude location. Some wizards state that the spell may only be performed in the far north of the world, when temperatures are below freezing. Some wizards conjecture that Ithaqua might be invoked from any high, snowy mountain, even those in the South Pole.
cost: Variable magic points; 1D10 Sanity points (caster only)

name: Call Nyogtha
description: Invokes the favor of Nyogtha, who appears to grant whatever the caster does if Nyogtha deems him worthy. The caster must perform the spell ritual at the entrance to any cavern (certain tomes hint that all great caverns lead to the same place where Nyogtha dwells).
cost: Variable magic points; 1D10 Sanity points (caster only)

name: Call Shah-Neklah
description: Calls Shah-Neklah—Nigguraths in a consecrated stone altar. This alter does not perform the sacrifice—the hunting horror serves the sacrifice. The caster must step away from the altar, speaking the spell backward, to bind the Horror.
cost: Variable magic points; 1D10 Sanity points (caster only)

name: Call Yog-Sothoth
description: Calls Yog-Sothoth to a specially constructed stone tower. The tower must be in an open area and the sky must be cloudless. The stone tower must be at least 30 feet high. Each cult has its own protocol; one must designate a human sacrifice for the god's taking. This need be no more than an invitation—gesturing toward a nearby person, for instance, from which Yog-Sothoth then selects a victim. At the cult's option, the stone tower may be enchanted, lowering the magic points needed for a successful cast of the spell.
cost: Variable magic points; 1D10 Sanity points (caster only)

name: Dismiss Deity
description: Every Dismiss spell differs: the caster must know the specific Dismiss spell for the particular deity. First, if no POW (rounded down) possessed by the deity is donated to the spell by a cultist, this grants an initial 5% chance to dismiss the god, and it opens the way for the deity's dismissal. The way once prepared, the sacrifice of more magic points can tempt the deity into departure. In this, the Dismiss spell is the reverse of the Call spell, though particular conditions or requirements may be unique.
cost: 1 minute plus 1 extra round per participant who donates magic points

name: Cause/Cure Blindness
description: The target suffers complete and permanent blindness, as if by a stroke. The same spell can reverse blindness if the eyes are not physically destroyed. The spell requires 8 magic points for either version. A day of ritual is required. For the spell to take effect the caster must succeed in an opposed POW roll with the target (automatic success if the target is consenting).
cost: 8 magic points; 1D6 Sanity points for Cause Blindness

name: Chant of Theth
description: The use of this spell increases the caster's mental faculties. Working his or her way to a string of particular intellectual problem. Spending 10 magic points in the casting enables the user to increase his or her INT roll to gain knowledge, learn a spell, translate a passage, deduce the meaning of a symbol, etc.

name: Cloud Memory
description:
The spell blocks the target's ability to consciously remember a particular event. The caster must be able to see the target and the target must be able to receive the caster's suggestion for this spell to have effect. Immediately if the caster succeeds in an opposed POW roll with the target it wins; the target's mind is mentally blocked regarding one specified incident. If the incident was terrifying, the victim may thereafter still have nightmares vaguely relating to it.
The caster cannot undo the spell from the target's mind—he or she must cite a certain event, such as "Forget you were assaulted by a monster." This spell cannot block knowledge of spells or of the Cthulhu Mythos unless the knowledge is firmly tied to a specific event, nor can it undo a Sanity loss or undo unconsciousness.

name: Clutch of Nyogtha
description:
The caster must spend 1 magic point to begin the spell and the target must be within conversational distance. For the spell to take effect the caster must succeed in an opposed POW roll with the target. If the caster wins the roll, the target feels as though an arm, hand or tentacle is crushing his or her heart, losing 1D3 hit points for each combat round that the spell is in effect. While under such attack, the target is constantly paralyzed, as though having a heart attack. In the round that the accumulated damage from this spell reduces his or her points to zero, the target's chest ruptures and bursts, and the target's smoking heart appears in the hand of the caster.
Around each round the spell lasts, the caster must spend magic points equal to twice the number of hit points in damage. The caster must concentrate on the spell each round that it is to remain in effect and the caster must defeat the target on an opposed POW roll each round. If the caster is distracted or if the target successfully resists, the spell ends. Any damage already done remains.
cost: 1+ magic points equal to twice the number of hit points in damage that round; 1D10 Sanity points

name: Consume Likewise
description: The caster can assume the living likeness of a person freshly dead, acquiring the victim's attributes, X-ray eyes, and so on. The caster assumes the being's living appearance, though which internal organs begin to replicate in the target. The experience of the spell is devastating; however, successful Psychoanalysis can help the victim come to terms with these applicable dreams and hallucinations, and restore half of each such Sanity loss.
cost: 10 magic points, every 6 hours of casting; 5 POW; 1D20 Sanity points

name: Contact Spells
description:
Using Contact spells a cultist could get in touch with monsters and alien races, perhaps to learn about magic, gods, or alien places. The caster should have a definite goal in mind; bargains might be struck and plans negotiated.
Contact spells do not give the caster the upper hand though; the spell is merely a method of establishing contact. Remember these are independent and intelligent beings with their own motivations—casters make contact at their peril. Knowing one Contact spell is of no help in casting another.
Cast properly this spell always works, unless there are no such things living within a convenient distance. If a contacted entity is large, such as a flying polyp, it may wish to swarm threatening, in order not to seem threatening. Once the contacted thing appears, it is free to depart, so if the caster has something to offer it, the chance for an extended meeting greatly improves.
cost: Relative to specific spell; Sanity cost: 1D3 (plus 1 or more to view the monster)

name: Contact Cthonian
description: Unless there are no chthonians nearby, it succeeds automatically. The spell must be cast at a location that has shown recent earthquakes or where hot spots and volcanoes offer relatively quick transport from the deep interior of the planet to the outer edge of the geologic crust.

name: Contact Deep One
description: Unless there are no deep ones nearby, the chanting succeeds automatically. This spell should be cast near the edge of a salt-water ocean. To work best, it should be cast near a deep one city, such as the waters off Innsmouth, Massachusetts or the coast near the town of Dunwich. Each version of the ritual insists on throwing specially inscribed stones into the water.

name: Contact Elder Thing
description: Unless there are no elder things nearby, the chanting succeeds automatically. The most likely locations are along the southern part of the Mid-Atlantic Ridge or over the geological trenches nearest Antarctica.

name: Contact Flying Polyp
description: Unless there are no flying polyps nearby, it succeeds automatically. Flying polyps have their rare underground cities, the largest of which is beneath the City in the Sands, in the western Australian desert. The caster should first construct or open a communication shell for their egress.

name: Contact Formless Spawn
description: Unless there are no formless spawn nearby, it succeeds automatically. The best place to cast is at a temple of Tsathoggua, whose altar statues of Tsathoggua is still present or at a spot near an opening into the earth with one of the following: a cave. The Oklahoma. Possibly other places in Western or Midwestern North America or caves northeast of points. One such cavern reportedly exists near Dunwich, Massachusetts.

name: Contact Ghoul
description: Unless there are no ghouls nearby, it succeeds automatically. Ghouls are found wherever large concentrations of humans are, especially near graveyards and crypts. Places of burial more than a century old are propitious locations for this spell. Moonlit nights are best.

name: Contact Gnaph-Keh
description: Unless there are no gnaph-keh nearby, it succeeds automatically. Gnaph-keh are usually found in Greenland or other frozen wastes near the North Pole. The spell must be augmented with a successful Sing (Art & Craft specialism) roll and before doing so the caster must build a small effigy of the beast from ice and snow.

name: Contact Hound of Tindalos
description: If a hound of Tindalos is summoned it will come automatically. There is no known way to argue with a hound of Tindalos, whose motive for moving through the stream of time and space is pure hunger.

name: Contact Mi-Go
description: Unless there are no mi-go nearby, it succeeds automatically. The spell must be cast at the base or the top of a high mountain—in a maze known to exist on the Plateau of Leng, for example. Among such ranges are the Appalachians, Andes and the Himalayas, and some peaks in Central Africa.

name: Contact Sand-Dweller
description: Unless there are no sand-dwellers nearby, it succeeds automatically. The spell must be cast in a suitably arid desert, such as in the Sahara, the southwestern United States, Saudi Arabia, or central Australia.

name: Contact Servitor of the Outer Gods
description: A successful Lock roll means that a servitor is within the Milky Way galaxy when the spell is cast. If no servitor is within this range, the magic points are lost. Stars may speak to the caster. He or she attempts to visualize Azathoth's Ineffable Heart (reputedly a spheroidal symbol of meditation but actually of unknown shape) that pulses and drums like liquid flame. The servitor manifests within 1D4+4 rounds and expects a blood sacrifice. If the sacrifice is inadequate, the servitor devours the caster. If the servitor accepts the sacrifice, it agrees to perform one simple task for the caster.

name: Contact Star-Spawn of Cthulhu
description: Unless there are no star-spawn of Cthulhu representatives nearby, it succeeds automatically. It should be cast at the ocean shore, perhaps near a deep one outpost or at the spot where star-spawn may sleep. The spell works well in Polynesia, along the Massachusetts coast, and above R'lyeh.

name: Contact Yithian
description: This spell does not reach forward or backward in time. If a Yithian mind occupies a subject within one hundred miles of the caster at the time the spell is cast, the Yithian notices the appeal and may respond if curious, fearful, or attentive. If the caster has nothing interesting to say or offer, then the Great Race scholar ignores him or her, or perhaps destroys the caster as a likely pest or nuisance.

name: Contact Deity Spells
description: Contact Deity spells are cast by the insane priests of these deities, or by great independent sorcerers who hope to strike deals and win over some small fraction of the deities' powers. Unlike Contact spells, Contact can be any one of the Gods or Great Old Ones. For each such spell, the caster must sacrifice 5 POW. The chance of successfully contacting the deity is equal to half the caster's (reduced) POW. If the roll succeeds, the deity or some aspect of it will contact the caster in a semi-friendly manner after a few game hours or days. If the deity is unlikely to give non-worshipers anything of value, if the deity offers to help the investigators it may try to squab the caster, or drive him or her insane, and so get some inhuman pleasure from the situation.

name: Contact Deity: Chaugnar Faugn
description: Opens communication with Chaugnar Faugn. With the spell wide open the caster visions or nightmares that inform the dreamer of the god's desires, or lets him or her know if a petition to the god is to be favorably answered or rejected.

name: Contact Deity: Cthulhu
description: Opens communication with Cthulhu. This can be cast from anywhere. Dreams and nightmares he informs his followers of his desires.

name: Contact Deity: Eihort
description: Resembles other Contact Deity spells, but upon successfully contacting Eihort the caster gains one bonus die on the opposed POW roll. By which: Eihort Taken by which: the Elder Sign they gain one bonus die on the opposed POW roll.

name: Contact Deity: Nodens
description: Opens communication with Nodens. The Contact for Nodens can be cast only from a remote, windswept place on the edge of a sea or high cliff. If later, Nodens contacts the caster, it will be when he or she is alone and in a similar inaccessible place.

name: Contact Deity: Nyarlathotep
description: This can be cast from anywhere. Nyarlathotep appears only at meetings of worshipers or when new priests of the Gods are anointed.

name: Contact Deity: Tsathoggua
description: Opens communication with Tsathoggua. The god contacts the caster through a hazy and translucent projection of his real self, to which normal Sanity losses apply. He usually visits only if the caster is alone. He speaks audibly to the caster.

name: Contact Deity: Y'golonac
description: This god responds by attempting to convert the caster to its worship. If the deity feels that the invoker would be an unsuitable priest, it will physically attack the caster and try to eat the soul and mind. If the caster has sufficient strength to resist, it may try to possess a friend, with a (controlled) approaches the caster to tempt him or her into service.

name: Create Barrier of Naach-Tith
description:
The barrier provides both a physical and magical defense. Each participant in this spell loses 1D10 Sanity points and a variable number of magic points. Each magic point expended in the creation provides the barrier 3D10 points of STR. To create the barrier, all magic points to be used must be expended and the resulting barrier lists 1D4+4 hours. Anyone who knows the spell may contribute magic points to the casting and contribute magic points to the barrier.
The barrier is trapezoidal in shape and about 100 yards in diameter. It can be cast around a character to protect from harm or it can be cast to encompass a monster or enemy. Any creature brought within the barrier's confines if the creation is unharmed and thrust outside the barrier. Anything trapped should be allowed a STR roll opposed to the barrier's STR; for example a simple creature cannot combine their STR to escape.

name: Create Mist of R'lyeh
description: The spell causes a dense mist to appear in an egg-shaped volume, in front of the caster. The long axis of the cloud is always at right angles to the surface. The mist makes visible magic lines extending from a place enchanted for the Calling of a Mythos deity, the aura around a Gate, or a creature that is normally invisible, such as a star vampire. Using the powder coats no Sanity points, but seeing what it reveals does.

name: Create Zombie
description: A ritual liquid must be poured over a corpse or into the grave where a corpse lies. The Keeper determines the ingredients of the liquid—at least one part should be difficult to obtain. The corpse is left to mature. At the end of a week, the caster returns to the grave and reanimates it as his or her tool. At the end of the half-hour chant, the corpse claws its way from the earth and thereafter obeys the caster's will. The zombie will perform mundane and simple tasks, using "grabs," "fetches," or "kill him!" The zombie must periodically receive replacements. Thus, a wizard wielding a constant zombie servant must periodically create replacements.

name: Curse of the Putrid Husk
description:
This spell brings sanity-wrecking illusions to the victim. The victim perceives that his or her skin is in visible rotting and corrupting. The outward appearance seems to deteriorate swiftly, so that great rents and tears occur through which internal organs begin to tumble out (with the victim losing 1D10 Sanity points in the process). Thereupon the victim faints and awakens in a few minutes, whole and normal.
For the spell to take effect an opposed INT roll must be made between the caster and the target—one-minus penalty die on the target if the spell is cast at night, while the target is sleeping. The entire cycle of effect takes about twenty minutes, including 1D10 rounds of concentration. The target must be known to the caster and the caster must have as a focus some personal object belonging to the target.

name: Death Spell
description: Reserved only for the most foul of sorcerers, this spell causes the bewitched victim to burst into flames. The target must be within ten yards of the caster. For the spell to take effect the caster must succeed in an opposed POW roll with the target during 1D3 rounds of concentration. After 1D3 rounds of concentration, the victim's skin forms large blisters and he or she loses 1D3 hit points. In the next round, the victim loses an additional 1D6 hit points. On the third round, the victim bursts into flames, losing 1D10 hit points then and each round thereafter. The stench of burning hair and flesh is overwhelming (all witnesses should make SAN rolls or lose 1/1D6 SAN). It is impossible to control, since the horrified victim burns from the inside out.

name: Dominate
description: Sends the will of the target to the caster. For the spell to take effect the caster must succeed in an opposed POW roll with the target. If successful, the target obeys the commands of the caster without exception until the spell ends. Any command is possible.

name: Dread Curse of Azathoth
description: The sorcerer draws upon the energies of the Outer God to directly drain POW from a victim. By repeating the secret name of Azathoth, one can generate respect and fear in the victim; while reeling from the energy blast, the caster makes the target lose the secret Last Syllable. Said alone, the Last Syllable may be directed against a foe. For the spell to take effect the caster must succeed in an opposed POW roll with the target. If the caster is successful, the target loses 5D6 POW.

name: Dust of Suleiman
description: Creates a grey-green powder that harms unnatural beings from other planes of existence. The powder requires the dust from an Egyptian mummy at least 2,000 years old. Sprinkled over a being of this Earth, the entity is unlikely to be or feel the dust. The powder works only on extraterrestrial and other planar beings; thus the powder would harm a deep one or shantak, or Yog-Sothoth or a mi-go, but it is useless against a shoggoth or a deep one.

name: Elder Sign
description: Its decoration was, rather, in the rough shape of a star, in the center of which was what appeared to be a caricature of a single giant eye; but it was not an eye, rather a broken lozenge in shape with curious lines suggestive of flames or perhaps a solitary pillar of flame. The spell activates an Elder Sign. Each active Elder Sign takes a sacrifice of 10 POW to create, but costs no Sanity. An Elder Sign may be formed into a leaden seal carved in rock, forged in steel, etc. When made active beside an opening or Gate, it makes that path impassable to minions of the Great Old Ones and Mythos Gods. The symbol itself means nothing and has no effect.

name: Enchant Book
description: Aids in summoning star vampires by enchanting any book in which is inscribed the words of the spell Summon/Bind Star Vampire. The book's ink must contain the ichor of a star vampire. The caster sacrifices a varying amount of POW, loses 1D4 Sanity points, and meditates for three days. For each point of POW channeled to the book, add 2 percentiles to the caster's chance of success with Summon/Bind Star Vampire.

name: Enchant Knife
description:
An enchanted knife increases the success chance for other spells. The spell enchants a knife or dagger made of a pure metal, such as gold or iron. With the knife the blade the caster is used to kill an animal of at least SIZ 20. The creature's blood is then traced out to follow the lines of the drawn diagram and the enchantment is complete.
Such a knife is suitable for use in casting Summon Dimensional Shambler. Every point of POW placed into the knife adds 1 percentile to the chance for success in casting that spell. It is also useful to slay the small animal for Enchant Sacrificial Dagger. Each knife adds 10 percentiles to the chance for successfully casting Conjure Glass of Murthan.

name: Enchant Pipes
description: Augments the chance of success in casting particular spells. The spell enchants a set of pipes or a flute. The pipes add 2 percentiles to the chance for success in casting a particular spell for each pipe or flute included in the set.

name: Enchant Sacrificial Dagger
description: A flame-shaped dagger over which this spell is performed. The dagger must then be used to kill a living being of at least 100 POW, consuming all results of the enchanter's own POW must be sacrificed. From then on, when the dagger is used properly to sacrifice chosen victims in the presence of Nyarlathotep, the POW of the victim flows into the caster to any convenient storage site—the caster or an inanimate object.

name: Enchant Whistle
description: Augments the caster's chance to Summon/Bind Byakhee. The spell enchants a whistle, which must be an alloy of silver and iron. For each point of POW sacrificed to the whistle, add 2 percentiles to the chance of successfully casting Summon/Bind Byakhee.

name: Enthrall Victim
description: The caster must be able to speak calmly with the target before the spell can take effect. After a round or so of talking, an opposed POW roll must be made between the caster and target on 1D100. If overcome, the victim stands struck, numb and dumb until relieved from the trance by a mental assault or some similarly shocking event. If the caster fails to overcome the target, he or she may try the spell the following round.

name: Evil Eye
description: In a successful casting, the victim's chance for a successful roll is halved; "Nothing is halved"; the victim cannot see or gain luck points if the Optional Luck Rule is being used. The victim must apply one penalty die to all characteristic rolls, and his or her guns jam on every roll of 77% or higher. The chances for Summon/Bind spells are also halved. The effect lasts until the next sunrise, until the caster removes the spell, until the caster is found and blinded (hit hard enough to bleed) or until the victim dies.

name: Find Gate
description: Through a series of gestures and a specific chant, the spell reveals to the caster any Gate if there is within the caster's direct vision. While this limited form of the spell allows the caster to find a Gate, it does not empower anyone to open, close, create, or pass through it. Neither does this limited form of the spell allow the caster to determine whether the Gate traverses time or distance.

name: Gate Boxes
description: Allows the creation of twin magical boxes that form two ends of a magical Gate. Going through a gate box costs exactly the same magic points and Sanity points as going through a regular Gate and the transit procedure is the same. First, two identical wooden boxes must be made to any desired SIZ. The boxes should be open only at one end. Then the necessary rituals must be performed for the creation of boxes and conceiving certain hyper-dimensional lines and angles in one's mind for about five hours must be performed. 5 POW is sacrificed at this time. Then the boxes automatically drain half the creator's Magic points each day, until they have absorbed magic points equal to their total SIZ x 10.
cost: 1 POW; variable magic points and Sanity points

name: Time Gate
description: The wizard is, through the use of diabolical symbols and chants, able to create multi-pathway Time Gates—traveling is possible through more than one distant time or future. Only the caster knows which path leads to which destination—those foolish enough to use the controlled drawn diagrams by the caster will be unable to determine the correct path and could arrive at any of the times the Gate is keyed to (roll randomly). The process of creation remains the same.

name: View Gate
description:
Creates a gate to the future or to the past. The spell resembles the Create Gate spell but costs POW for the number of years traveled rather than the number of miles journeyed. Use the Gate Creation and Travel Costs table and substitute distance in years for distance in miles.
The spell only yields a time approximate to what is desired, though once such a Gate is created the exact interval between 'now' and 'then' remains the same.

name: Green Decay
description: A curse that turns an individual into a green pile of mold. A day must be spent focusing energy upon the intended victim. The ritual requires the investment of energy. During the next 24 hours the caster must personally give a green clod to the target. At dawn on each of the following days the target effectively turns into a green mold. At first green blotches appear on the skin. These then begin to grow mold-like hairs. As the days progress, more and more of the victim's skin grows green. By the sixth day the victim's mental facilities rapidly deteriorate and movement becomes impossible. The only known solution is to find and kill the caster, which reverses the spell's effects.

name: Fist of Yog-Setheth
description:
The caster causes a massive, invisible force to hit a single target. The caster must be able to see the target. Each magic point invested in the spell yields 2D10 STR of force. For every 30 feet of distance that the caster stands from the target (after the first thirty feet), the caster must spend an additional magic point.
When the target is struck (a living thing), an opposed roll must be made using the spell's STR versus the target's CON. If successful, the target is knocked unconscious. Whether or not the target is left unconscious, the target is always pushed away from the caster for a distance in feet equal to the STR of the blow minus the target's SIZ.

name: Flesh Ward
description: Grants protection against physical attack. Each magic point spent gives the caster (or the chosen target) 1D6 points of armor against non-magical attacks. This protection wears off as it blocks damage. For example, if a character had 12 points of Flesh Ward as armor and was hit for 8 points of damage, the Flesh Ward would be reduced to 4 points, yet he or she would take no damage. The spell lasts 8 hours until the protection is used up. Once cast, the spell may not be reinforced with further magic points, nor recast until the old spell's protection has been used up.

name: Implant Fear
description: This spell causes soul-chilling dread, breaking the target's concentration and disrupting his or her emotions. The caster must be able to see and point a finger at the target within 30 seconds. The sudden unnerving costs the target 0/1D6 Sanity points and they feel compelled to retreat to a safe place—introductory to safety, as they would normally feel. The caster adds 15 percentile to the chance for a success with a daguer, and 2 percentiles to the chance for successfully casting Conjure Glass of Murthan.

name: Melt Flesh
description: Costs 1 magic point per 15 SIZ of dead flesh; 1D4 Sanity points. For living flesh, costs 5 magic points per 15 SIZ of living flesh; 1D4 Sanity points. The caster must be within 30 yards of the target. This diabolical spell heats flesh (dead or living) to its melting point, causing tremendous damage. In certain cases, such as where the face is targeted, the victim may become blind or permanently disfigured. Seeing human flesh or all of human bones costs 1/1D6 Sanity points, unless air vents can be quickly created.
cost: 1 magic point per 15 SIZ of dead flesh; 5 magic points per 15 SIZ of living flesh; 1D4 Sanity points

name: Mental Suggestion
description:
The caster can command any being with human blood in its veins. A single target, who is visible to the unassisted eye of the sorcerer, is affected. The victim carries out all commands as the caster, even to self-destruction. The spell also sends suggestions to targeting Lengs, deep one hybrids, ghoul changelings, Little People, serpent folk and deep ones who have interbred with humans, but doesn't work on nonhuman entities such as shantaks or dimensional shamblers.
The caster intones the spell and relates the suggestion to the target. For the spell to take effect the caster must succeed in an opposed POW roll with the target. Extreme suggestions (such as death or great harm) require a second opposed POW roll just before the suggestion is implemented.
cost: Variable magic points and Sanity points

name: Mind Exchange
description:
Allows the caster to trade minds with another individual. The spell costs 1D3 Sanity points and costs magic points equal to one-sixth of the target's POW the first time the spell is cast. Thereafter: the cost goes down 1 magic point per casting until it reaches 1, where it remains. The Sanity point cost does not decrease.
The target must know the spell caster and love or strongly care for him or her. If that affection is somehow lost, the exchange can no longer occur. Fresh affection renews the chance. Upon arriving in another's body, the target loses a minimum of 1/1D3 Sanity points.

name: Mind Transfer
description: A powerful spell allowing the caster to permanently trade minds with a target, perhaps to attain longer life at the target's expense. For the spell to take effect the caster must succeed in an opposed POW roll with the target. With success, the caster has permanently taken control of the target's body. The caster's previous body is now a hollow dead thing. As for the mind of the target, which had been expelled from the body and thus dies; however some hint that it remains inside the corpse as a conscious mind and is locked in a permanent state of horrific madness.

name: Mirror of Tarkhun Atep
description: As a harassment or warning, the caster is able to project his or her image onto the surface of a mirror, or mirroring object, that is being gazed into by the caster's intended victim. The victim can be anywhere on Earth. The caster needs a mirror with a minimum of 1 magic point and 1 Sanity point to cast the spell. Viewing a vampire or other reflective surface, an image of the caster begins to appear in the mirror. When the target next looks into a darkened window, a mirror, or any other reflective surface, an image of the caster begins to appear in it. The image then begins to speak—if the caster is seen standing behind the victim in the reflection, if the victim wears the talisman, it is reflected from the senses may even be resolved. The target can hear words and short phrases uttered by the caster. The caster can see the reflection of the mirror as well, and see the target and the target's surroundings.

name: Mindblast
description: The caster of this spell unleashes a terrible mental assault, causing the loss of 5 Sanity points and inducing temporary insanity. For the spell to take effect the caster must succeed in an opposed POW roll with the target. The target is affected as per the rules for temporary insanity, and may continue to suffer from flashbacks and/or nightmares for days following the incident.

name: Powder of Ibn-Ghazi
description: This spell makes visible magically invisible things by throwing the powder against or onto the invisible thing. The powder must be compounded carefully, according to exacting instructions—at least two people participated and the sacrifice of 1 magic point per dose. A dose is one application of the powder. The Keeper determines the special ingredients. That which could be made visible (include the magical lines extending from a place enchanted for the Calling of a Mythos deity, the aura around a Gate, or a creature that is normally invisible, such as a star vampire. Using the powder coats no Sanity points, but seeing what it reveals does.

name: Red Sign of Shudde M'ell
description:
An awful spell that causes one or several victims to die horribly. The caster inscribes in the air the dread sign of Shudde M'ell, while speaking words of power. When formed correctly, a dull red symbol glows in the air as the caster's finger describes it. The victims' malevolent effects manifest the round after its creation. Once formed, the sign must be maintained by concentration and the expenditure of 3 more magic points each round.
All those within 10 yards of the sign lose 1D3 hit points per round as their bodies quake and spasm, and their internal organs and blood vessels convulse. Those further than 10 yards but nearer than 30 yards lose 1 hit point each round. Those further than 30 yards take no damage.

name: Resurrection
description: He turned from the inscriptions to face the room with its bizarre contents, and saw that the lights on the floor, by which the enormous efflorescent pools had last been, lying forth a cloud of thick, greenish-black vapour of smothering volume and opacity. The spell reduces a corpse to its essential salts and compounds, a bluish-grey powder, or reverses the process to yield afterwards the form and soul of the deceased. A written copy of this spell is known to be in a great haunted house on a great offering the star Calamno, one of the Pleiades.

name: Separate Binding
description: Binding and Summoning are two sides of the same coin. Sometimes the Keeper may be asked if the effects may be combined or separated. In which might be asked for, one to summon and another to bind.

name: Shrivelling
description: A powerful offensive spell causing physical injury to a target. The caster invests as many magic points as desired and that number halved in Sanity points. For the spell to take effect the caster must succeed in an opposed POW roll with the target. If the roll is successful, the target suffers a sudden and terrifying blast of energy, causing flesh to blacken and wither. The target loses 1 hit point per magic point invested in the spell.
cost: Variable Sanity points (half the magic points spent)

name: Song of Hastur
description: A hideous wailing ululation by the caster causes the skin and flesh of the chosen victim to bubble and leister into pestilent blisters. The target must be visible to the caster. Though everyone can hear the song, the spell will affect only the person chosen as target. If the King in Yellow is visible, a roll equal to or under the caster's POW as a roll on 1D100 is required. Successfully cast, the spell causes the target 1D6 hit points damage per round. After every four rounds, internal ruptures lower the victim's CON by 3D10. When victim's CON reaches 0 or death occurs from hit point loss, the body swells up and bursts with a sickening—pop!—as steamy gore spills onto the floor.
cost: 1D4 magic points per round; 1D4 Sanity points per round

name: Form of the Command
description: The caster's command to the thing must be specific and limited in duration: 'proceed to my cellar' or 'leave town.' A command cannot, however, say 'stay in my service forever.' Keep commands simple. The best rule of thumb is that a command should be no more words than the thing's INT. Simple gestures such as pointing will be understood. Assume that the thing is always able to understand a simple command, whether spoken in English or Urdu. See also Separate Binding.

name: Summoning Spells
description:
Such spells concern themselves with alien races and attendants, those monsters that commonly serve greater monsters or wizards. The general procedures for these spells are the same, but conditions may vary from spell to spell. Knowing one such spell is of no use whatsoever in attempting to cast another. Unless the Keeper wishes otherwise, the summons and the bind portions of each spell are learned together. Foolish is the imprudent wizard who only learns to summon and not to bind.
With the exception of Summon/Bind Servitor of the Outer Gods, these spells require the sacrifice of 1 magic point per 10 percentile chance for success. For example, 3 magic points gives a 30% chance for the spell to succeed. In general, for each magic point spent, the caster must spend one minute chanting—the longer the chance for success, the longer the spell takes to cast. A result of 96-100 is always a failure—a roll of 100 should always have had consequences for the wizard concerned. The caster also loses 1D4 Sanity points per spell cast, whether the spell succeeds or not.
cost: Variable magic points; 1D4 Sanity points per magic point spent

name: Summon Byakhee
description: A single Byakhee appears approaching from a great distance. The magic point cost is triple the normal summoning spell; the chance for a successful cast adds 10 percentiles for each three magic points spent. This spell may be cast only at night in the open air. If no astronomical preparation is prepared for the binding, see Separate Binding. If the flute is enchanted, it increases the chance for the spell's success by 2 percentiles per point of POW within the book. This spell can be cast only at night under a cloudless sky.
cost: Variable magic points (cost is triple normal); 1D4 Sanity points

name: Summon Dimensional Shambler
description: A single dimensional shambler is summoned. A creature to be bound must be visible to the caster and within 100 yards. Binding requires an opposed roll and opposed rolls cannot be pushed; if the opposed roll is failed, the caster is in trouble! If an enchanted whistle is used, the caster gains a bonus die on the opposed POW roll. How the flute is treated is up to the caster.

name: Summon Fire Vampire
description: Causes one fire vampire to come down from the sky like a shimmering heat. The caster must have Fomalhaut above the horizon. One bonus die is gained if the caster uses an enchanted dagger made from pure metal such as copper or iron: they gain one bonus die to the opposed POW roll. How the flute is treated is up to the caster. A fire vampire summoned by this spell gains one bonus die on the opposed POW roll if the caster stands in a bonfire.

name: Summon Hunting Horror
description: A single horror appears, seemingly formed from the constriction of the night and a sharp form of the black flying horror appears. To summon, a stone is needed with the cryptic carvings like liquid flame. The servitor need not be star-shaped. The spell can be cast only at night when there is no moon.

name: Summon Nightgaunt
description: A nearly silent swoosh of air comes, and then the single ghostly form of the black-faced flying horror appears. To summon, a stone is needed with the cryptic carvings like liquid flame. The servitor need not be star-shaped. The spell can be cast only at night when there is no moon.

name: Summon Servitor of the Outer Gods
description: If a monster arrives unbidden or is come upon unexpectedly, it may be bound on the spot. The caster must know the Summon/Bind spell for that type of thing. For the spell to take effect the thing must be turned into the bond. For the spell to take effect the caster must succeed in an opposed POW with the target. Each cast of the binding works on only one creature at a time. One option open to the caster is to invest a number of magic points equal to one-tenth of the summoned creature's POW to gain a bonus die on the opposed POW roll to bind the creature; thus the caster must take a gamble on how many magic points to sacrifice.

name: Summon Star Vampire
description: Curious rustling sounds are heard and the smell of damp earth fills the nose of those nearby. The caster must have a source of fire present to summon a star vampire. If the caster uses an enchanted book containing the spell, the caster gains a bonus die on the opposed POW roll. One bonus die may be gained if the caster stands in a bonfire. Enchanting a book in which are written or inscribed the words of the spell, the chance for success is increased by 2 percentiles per point of POW within the book.

name: Blind Servitor of the Outer Gods
description: If the caster possesses an enchanted dagger made from any pure metal such as copper or iron they gain one bonus die to the opposed POW roll. How the caster stands in a bonfire if the servitor is exposed to bright sunlight.

name: Blind Star Vampire
description: If the caster possesses an enchanted book containing the spell they gain one bonus die on the opposed POW roll. How the flute is treated is up to the caster.
cost: 1 magic point per die of star vampire's attacks

name: Veerish Sign
description: This from outside the universe, from outside of which they come—those ones that are assembled must not be followed and are not to exist here. Though they have come from outside the stars and swim the void between, the Veerish Sign made in the air in front of them will give one second's pause. An ancient and powerful haunt-ward that benefits the casting of other spells. It may lessen the cost, reduce the casting time or increase the effect a little, and the entire spell in question. Veerish Sign is also rumored to temporarily ward against Mythos monsters—such creatures are wary of the Sign and hesitant to attack while the wizard's intentions are known.

name: Warding
description: A number of ordinary white stones are placed on the ground on the outer perimeter of an area. They must be within a yard of each of the others. When cast, a shimmering heat haze can be seen over the stones. Thereafter if any creature moves through the warding line, the fact, even if asleep until then. Once this has occurred, the spell is broken.

name: Wave of Oblivion
description: A mighty spell that causes a powerful ocean wave to smash down at the caster's direction. The caster must be able to see the intended target and sufficient ocean or any salt water must be present to create the wave. The caster stands in the water to cast the spell. The wave is 3,000 cubic feet, enough to swamp or capsize a small loop. Needless to say, people swallowed up by such waves vanish forever below the surface. Others can contribute magic points as desired and must expend as many magic points as desired and must expend 1D8 Sanity points.

name: Wither Limb
description: A terrifying spell designed to torment and cause permanent damage to a target. The caster must be within 10 yards of the target. For the spell to take effect the caster must succeed in an opposed POW roll with the target. The said appendage rapidly and painfully withers and shrivels, causing 1D6 hit points of damage and a permanent loss of 3D10 CON. The victim loses 1D4/1D8 Sanity points and they feel compelled to retire to a safe place—introductory to safety as they would normally feel.

name: Words of Power
description: The caster creates an intense bond with a large group of people. The caster must speak extemporaneously and seemingly from the heart, maintaining the audience's attention. For each ten minutes of speech, the caster makes a Communication roll so that each must make a communication roll as the Keeper thinks appropriate (Charm or Persuade), or the speech ends indecisively and the audience breaks up. If successful, each new magic point sacrificed thoroughly believes what has been said for 1D3 days.

name: Wrack
description: This spell will temporarily incapacitate a single target who is within 30 yards of the caster. For the spell to take effect the caster must succeed in an opposed POW roll with the target. The target's face and hands blister and drip fluid, and the eyes cloud with blood and become temporarily sightless. The target is reaching toward the caster before it normalizes. It lasts 1D6 rounds, after which night returns in 3D10 minutes, the target fully recovers and can resume normal activity.
